title: China should not have ... : Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang after share falls to zero in Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: ‘Lafda ho gaya na’: Ashwin’s cheeky reply on Rohit rift rumours
content: Ravichandran Ashwin playfully addressed fan speculation about a rift with Rohit Sharma after missing the LSG vs MI IPL 2026 clash. He clarified his absence was due to prior commentary commitments and travel, not any personal issue. Ashwin lauded Rohit's spectacular 84-run innings, despite the match's limited impact on both teams' playoff hopes.

title: As Dell CEO moves company’s base to Texas; gets message from Governor Greg Abott
content: Dell Technologies is moving its legal home from Delaware to Texas. This change is set for approval at the company's annual stockholders meeting on June 25, 2026. Texas Governor Greg Abbott welcomed the tech giant, highlighting its long history in the state.

title: 'More important ...': SC rebukes Centre, refuses to adjourn hearing on EC appointments law
content: The Supreme Court refused to postpone hearings on petitions challenging the 2023 law for appointing election commissioners, deeming the matter "more important than any other matter." The court allowed petitioners to begin arguments, emphasizing the significance of the case over other ongoing constitutional benches.

title: IPL: Chahal scripts unwanted record thanks to Punjab Kings’ butter fingers
content: Punjab Kings’ poor fielding left Yuzvendra Chahal frustrated during their clash against Sunrisers Hyderabad as dropped catches denied him crucial wickets. Shashank Singh dropped Heinrich Klaasen, while Lockie Ferguson spilled Ishan Kishan, both off Chahal’s bowling. PBKS have now dropped six catches off Chahal this season, the most for any bowler in IPL 2026, hurting Punjab badly on a batting-friendly Hyderabad pitch.

title: Microsoft's Xbox CEO brings execs from CoreAI engg group to gaming unit: What she said in memo
content: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma is shaking up leadership, bringing in AI experts from Microsoft's CoreAI to boost speed and community engagement. This significant overhaul sees new appointments and the departure of long-serving executives. Sharma emphasizes a need for faster innovation and deeper connection with gamers, aiming to revitalize the gaming division amidst recent hardware revenue drops.

title: Sooryavanshi at centre of bizarre ‘child labour’ FIR threat against RR
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is making waves in IPL 2026. His incredible batting skills have drawn attention. A social activist has raised concerns about child exploitation. He plans legal action against Rajasthan Royals. The activist believes the young player is a victim of child labor. Sooryavanshi's performances have been historic. He is the youngest T20 centurion.

title: Simone Biles 'in therapy' as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Vikings eye ex-Ravens $5M WR to fix offense after tough season
content: The Minnesota Vikings have had a pretty brutal run on the field in the last season after a pretty impressive performance in the 2024 season, sparking concern among fans. With 14 wins and 3 losses in the 2024 season, the Minnesota Vikings ended up recording just 9 wins and 8 losses in the previous season. Ahead of the new season, the team remains focused on fixing the roster.

title: Connor McDavid unwinds at Amangiri with Lauren Kyle after exit
content: After a loss to the Anaheim Ducks last night, Connor McDavid and Lauren Kyle are taking some time off to enjoy the pampering at Amangiri during their offseason. In the latest NHL news roundup, the blog catches up on his activities during the bye weeks, and they discuss the team’s next move.

title: AIADMK rules out extending support to Vijay’s TVK to form govt in TN
content: Munusamy stated that the media was spreading misinformation suggesting that the AIADMK would extend support to Vijay’s party.

title: ‘Sharam karo... Pant se kuch seekho’: Yuvraj teases Abhishek, Gill - Watch
content: Rishabh Pant gifted Yuvraj Singh a golf club and heartfelt note to thank him for mentoring him ahead of IPL 2026 after a difficult phase in his career. Sharing the gesture online, Yuvraj jokingly teased Shubman Gill, Abhishek Sharma and other mentees, saying, “Sharam karo... Rishabh Pant se kuch seekho.” Meanwhile, Pant continues to struggle for form in IPL 2026.

title: SP cuts tie-up with I-PAC: Akhilesh Yadav cites fund crunch ahead of 2027 UP polls
content: Samajwadi Party leader Akhilesh Yadav announced the party has ended its association with political consultancy I-PAC, citing financial constraints rather than electoral performance. Yadav clarified that the decision was due to limited resources, not recent election outcomes, and that the party had engaged I-PAC for early preparations for the 2027 Uttar Pradesh Assembly elections.

title: Sports Ministry reiterates policy on India vs Pakistan sporting relations
content: Sports Ministry has reaffirmed its policy of a blanket ban on bilateral sporting ties with Pakistan, with no Indian teams participating in Pakistan and vice-versa. However, exemptions remain for multi-nation events like the Olympics and Asian Games. The ministry also aims to simplify visa processes to position India as a preferred international sports hosting destination.

title: Ranbir Kapoor urged Neetu Kapoor to return amid India-Pakistan conflict
content: Shooting 'Daadi Ki Shaadi' in the picturesque backdrop of Shimla created lasting friendships among the cast, even as uncertain political tensions simmered between India and Pakistan. Neetu Kapoor reminisced about the joyful moments on set, likening it to her cherished experiences during 'Kabhi Kabhie,' where the absence of vanity vans encouraged genuine camaraderie.

title: IPL: Top 5 expert reactions to Sanju Samson’s match-winning 87* vs DC
content: Sanju Samson played a match-winning unbeaten knock of 87 runs. He led Chennai Super Kings to a comfortable eight-wicket victory against Delhi Capitals. Samson's composed innings, supported by Kartik Sharma, chased down the target with ease. This crucial win keeps CSK in contention for the IPL 2026 playoffs. Experts praised Samson's form and leadership.

title: Mike Vrabel wife Jennifer rethinks marriage amid Diana Russini scandal
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini, the former New York Times’ popular NFL insider and reporter, continue to be the eye of the storm as their affair rumors refuse to die down, sparking chaos. While Mike Vrabel has resumed his regular life after taking a short break, it seems his wife, Jennifer Vrabel has made a decision about their future.

title: A diary, a threat, and a $30bn stake: What Musk vs OpenAI trial has exposed
content: Seven trial days into Musk v. Altman, neither side is winning. Elon Musk filibustered his way through cross-examination. OpenAI president Greg Brockman's own diary—"what will take me to $1B?"—became the prosecution's best exhibit. A $97.4 billion bid surfaced through a passed note. And Demis Hassabis, the man who never took the stand, turns out to haunt every page of evidence.

title: Anupam Kher’s witty reply to Samik Bhattacharya’s ‘baal’ remark
content: Actor Anupam Kher humorously responded to a viral remark by West Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya, who used Kher's bald head as a metaphor for impossibility. Bhattacharya stated that the Trinamool Congress returning to power was as unlikely as hair growing back on Kher's head.

title: KSCA reacts to Bengaluru losing IPL 2026 final: ‘Specific reasons not communicated’
content: Bengaluru's M. Chinnaswamy Stadium has lost the rights to host the IPL 2026 final to Ahmedabad, much to the Karnataka Cricket Association's disappointment. Despite claiming full preparedness and a strong operational record, the KSCA was informed of the shift due to unspecified 'operational and logistical considerations' by the BCCI, sparking debate.

title: Samson not in peak physical condition, says Manjrekar; cites Kohli example
content: Sanjay Manjrekar urges Sanju Samson to emulate Virat Kohli's fitness dedication for sustained success. Despite Samson's stellar IPL 2026 season with two centuries and over 400 runs for CSK, Manjrekar believes the 31-year-old wicketkeeper-batter can enhance his physical conditioning. He highlighted Kohli's longevity, attributing it to prioritizing fitness to complement his skills and prevent physical limitations.

title: Video: CPM claims BJP 'goons attacked, broke Lenin statue' in Bengal's Murshidabad
content: Vladimir Lenin was a Russian revolutionary who led the 1917 Bolshevik Revolution and founded the Soviet Union, becoming one of the most influential communist leaders, having overwhelming influence in West Bengal politics under the CPM regime, which was uprooted by the TMC's Mamata Banerjee after three decades in 2011. The party said it registered a police complaint, leading to arrest of five and vowed to rebuild the statue on May 8.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Will one-third of the world be left in the dark?
content: Millions in Asia, including India, China, and Thailand, face uncertainty over watching the FIFA World Cup 2026 due to delayed broadcast rights. Unfavorable late-night match timings and high costs are stalling deals, potentially impacting viewership. Experts, however, believe agreements will be reached, ensuring widespread access to the global football spectacle.

title: The 2K-vote gap: How 12 narrow losses cost TVK simple majority
content: Actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam narrowly missed a majority, losing 12 seats by under 2,000 votes, including one by just 285 votes. Despite these close calls, TVK secured significant victories, with some margins exceeding 60,000 votes, demonstrating a strong debut performance.

title: 'IPL lines everyone’s pockets': Alastair Cook's fresh dig at Kevin Pietersen over Jacob Bethell debate
content: Alastair Cook reignited his debate with Kevin Pietersen over Jacob Bethell's IPL stint. Cook believes the young batter needs game time in county cricket over sitting on the bench in India. Pietersen countered, asserting Cook lacks understanding of the IPL's developmental benefits. Cook reiterated his stance, emphasizing playing matches over observation, despite the IPL's financial allure.

title: 'Blasts keep happening in India, nothing new': Farooq Abdullah downplays Punjab explosions
content: Twin explosions in Punjab's Jalandhar and Amritsar have sparked political debate, with former Union minister Farooq Abdullah downplaying the incidents and Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann accusing the BJP of fear-mongering. The NIA is assisting in the investigation of the blasts, which caused property damage but no casualties.

title: Andhra Pradesh: Man feeds poison-laced sweets to wife, smothers her
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Proddatur, Andhra Pradesh, where a husband allegedly murdered his wife by poisoning her. Driven by marital discord and an extramarital affair, the man reportedly purchased a potent substance online from a YouTuber for a hefty sum.

title: CJI was only meant to have a say in CEC/EC appointments till Parliament brought a law: Supreme Court
content: A nine-judge constitution bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant is currently hearing petitions regarding discrimination against women at religious sites, including the Sabarimala temple in Kerala, as well as the scope of religious freedom across various faiths

title: North Kerala districts could get fair representation in UDF Cabinet
content: Forty-five of the UDF’s 102 MLA-designates are from Kasaragod, Wayanad, Kannur, Kozhikode, Malappuram and Palakkad districts

title: Cobra, Rat Snake dominate 76% of rescues across 232 Hyderabad hotspots
content: A CCMB study found that spectacled cobras and Indian rat snakes account for 76% of urban snake rescues in Hyderabad, with encounters rising steadily and peaking during the monsoon

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: criticism against Pinarayi Vijayan in CPI meet following poll debacle
content: CPI plans to move forward by learning from the defeat and correcting what needs to be corrected, says party State secretary Binoy Viswam

title: Odisha fire service pulls off an unusual rescue – observed by a surprisingly tranquil python
content: A boy was trapped in a rock crevice for seven hours; a python that happened to be there too did not harm him

title: India to host first Big Cat Summit in June
content: Summit likely to adopt Delhi Declaration on shared priorities, landscape-based cooperative approach to conservation of seven big cat populations and their habitats; representatives of 95 countries to attend

title: Advertisement props Kodinhi, village of twins, to national limelight
content: The advert of an e-commerce giant, which shows the Malappuram village’s twins in light-hearted situations, goes viral. The village, with 2,000 families, has nearly 400 pairs of twins.

title: UDF’s near three-fourths majority marked a historic victory for the Congress-led coalition
content: Haemorrhaging vote shares across regions, the CPI(M)-led LDF registers its lowest vote share tally since 1982; Congress and IUML led the UDF surge with the former winning 63 seats, and the latter securing 22.

title: Rajnath Singh to address joint commanders’ meet in Jaipur
content: According to the Integrated Defence Staff, the conference will focus on enhancing military capabilities in emerging domains such as cyber, space and cognitive warfare

title: T.K. Govindan’s visits Kodiyeri’s family; Vinodhini says he is not a traitor
content: “Govindan is not stranger to us. We treat visitors with respect. Decisions regarding the party should be taken by  party leaders,” says Vinodini Kodiyeri, wife of the late CPI(M) leader Kodiyeri Balakrishnan

title: Elephant incursions into higher-elevation towns in The Nilgiris trigger alarm
content: If elephants starts frequenting Udhagamandalam town, they will have to rely on crop raiding as there is little natural fodder in the highly degraded forests of the upper plateau; they might even resort to foraging in garbage dumps, say expert

title: AIADMK rules out support to TVK
content: The tone for the reconsideration of its stance was set on Tuesday by Leema Rose, the newly elected legislator from Lalgudi, who told the media that negotiations were in progress between AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami and the TVK over government formation

title: Govt. revokes suspension of IPS officer K. Ramachandra Rao
content: Following his reinstatement, Mr. Rao has been posted as Director General of Police (Police Manual) with immediate effect

title: Seven panchaloha idols recovered from temple well in Palnadu; ASI to examine antiquity
content: Idols of Lord Venugopala Swamy, Rukmini Devi, Satyabhama, Lord Venkateswara Swamy, Sridevi, Bhudevi and Godadevi found submerged in a well at Sri Bhavannarayana Swamy temple in Inumella village; recovered during preliminary work for temple reconstruction

title: Congress has adopted BJP’s shortcuts in Tamil Nadu, says T.R. Baalu
content: “Congress can support anyone; it is their right. But political ethics demand that they remain faithful to the people who voted for them,” says DMK treasurer

title: CPI(M) to provide platform for party members at all levels to voice opinions to analyse causes of defeat
content: Decision to provide a platform for party members down to the branch committee level to express their opinions comes in the wake of crushing defeats for the party even in some of its strongholds in Kannur

title: CBI files chargesheet in builder-bank nexus case
content: The agency alleged a conspiracy by public servants and other private persons to induce homebuyers and investors through false assurances and fraudulent representations

title: A.P. govt. removes 51 doctors from services for remaining absent from duties
content: The doctors who faced action include 41 Assistant Professors, eight Associate Professors and two tutors, says Health Minister

title: Ongole Municipal Corporation plans events for 150th anniversary celebrations
content: Prakasam District Collector directs the officials to conduct a series of events to create awareness on cleanliness in the city

title: Dominance across two regions helps BJP-led bloc secure landslide victory in Assam | Data
content: The BJP-led North-East Democratic Alliance wins 102 of 126 seats, while the Congress-led Asom Sonmilito Morcha is reduced to 21; AIUDF secures two seats and the Trinamool Congress one

title: TVK changes Tamil Nadu’s political landscape | Data
content: Actor C. Joseph Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam has, in its electoral debut, decimated the DMK’s seat base in the urban heartlands where the ruling party had been strongest. The AIADMK held its ground in the west and north through coalition cohesion, and the DMK clung on best in its delta heartland.

title: Congress has adopted BJP’s shortcuts in Tamil Nadu, says T. R. Baalu
content: T.R. Baalu said the Congress’s statement outlining its political stand had exposed its “true character,” which, he claimed, had remained dormant at times

title: Pramod Sawant inaugurates road underbridge at Vasco-da-Gama
content: The new facility serves as a vital link connecting Vasco city with Dabolim Airport, Madgaon, and other parts of South Goa

title: Plea in Madras High Court insists on FIR, money laundering probe against TVK’s Vijay over 2015 income tax dispute
content: The Registry had refused to number the case last month; however it got numbered on Wednesday (May 6, 2026) following a judicial direction to leave the question of maintainability to be decided by the judges

title: India, Vietnam review defence ties; push for deeper maritime, industrial cooperation
content: The two sides signed as many as 13 agreements which will provide for deeper two-way engagement in a range of areas

title: TTD doubles dhupa, deepa and naivedyam aid for temples in A.P.
content: ₹43.40 crore allocated for infra development at TTD-run educational institution, ₹6 crore for a water pipeline; appointment of Dr. Shobha Raju as Asthana Vidwan cleared;  centralised administrative complex proposed at Tirumala

title: Police ramp up action against alleged loan app networks
content: Fresh evidence points to organised cyber extortion networks operating behind alleged loan apps; multiple complaints highlight prolonged harassment, data misuse and intimidation of borrowers

title: "Paid High Price For Loyalty": Upset DMK Slams Congress Over Vijay Tie-Up
content: Recounting how it has stood by the Congress through thick and thin, the DMK has now accused it of being "opportunists."

title: 'If They Don't Agree, Bombing Starts': Trump Threatens Iran Amid Deal Buzz
content: US officials have so far said little about Allen's alleged motivation, pointing only to an email Allen sent to relatives on the night of the attack.

title: DMK Negotiations And A Call To Vijay: Behind Congress-TVK Alliance
content: While everyone was taken aback by the TVK's resounding victory, some leaders within Tamil Nadu Congress had sensed this "Vijay tsunami" well in advance.

title: Meet Governor RV Arlekar, At The Helm Of Tamil Nadu's Big Transition
content: Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar carries with him a political journey that stretches from the dusty RSS shakhas of post-liberation Goa to the constitutional corridors of south India

title: Vijay Falling Short Of Numbers, 30 AIADMK MLAs Raring To 'Help'
content: Sources today said around 30 of AIADMK's 47 MLAs are in favour of joining hands with Vijay, but party chief E Palaniswami is reluctant.

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran, China Envoys Meet As Trump Pauses US Effort In Hormuz
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

title: Hantavirus Outbreak Kills 3 In Atlantic Cruise Ship : What We Know So Far
content: Hantavirus is a rare disease that usually spreads from infected rodents, typically through urine, droppings and saliva.

title: SpaceX IPO To Give Elon Musk More Power In Company, Curb Shareholder Rights
content: SpaceX's IPO registration statement sharply limits investor's ability to challenge management, sue in court and force votes on governance issues.

title: As Trump-Xi Summit Draws Near, China Steps Up Iran War Diplomacy
content: China is not an official mediator in the US-Iran war, but it has played an important role in efforts to de-escalate the conflict.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Dam Breaks: Kerala Didn't Reject The Left, It Rejected What It Had Turned Into
content: This verdict is not merely an anti-incumbency wave, but also a moral correction issued by a politically literate electorate against the tone of power.

title: Scammer Poses As Rahul Gandhi's Assistant, Cheats Woman Of Rs 25 Lakh
content: The accused, who claimed to be the PA of Rahul Gandhi, gained her trust by playing audio clips mimicking the voices of multiple political leaders.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Mafia, Scams and Shifting Loyalties: How Mamata Banerjee Lost The Plot
content: It was not a single misstep. It was the cumulative weight of multiple changes - some gradual, some sudden - that altered the outcome.

title: Iran War Anger May Have Triggered Trump Dinner Shooting Suspect: Report
content: According to a report by Reuters, a preliminary assessment by the US Department of Homeland Security's Office of Intelligence and Analysis last month concluded that the Iran war "may have contributed to his decision to conduct the attack".

title: Punjab Congress, BJP On Same Side, Take On Bhagwant Mann Over Blast Comment
content: Two low-intensity blasts took place on Tuesday night -- one outside the Border Security Force's Punjab Frontier headquarters in Jalandhar and another near the army cantonment area in Amritsar

title: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live: Vijay Wasn't Able To Prove He Has Numbers For Majority: Sources
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: The TVK emerged as the single-largest party with 108 seats in the 234-member assembly, falling short of the majority mark of 118.

title: Assam Cop Shot In Anti-Drug Raid Near Manipur Border, Airlifted To Guwahati
content: Police said no arrests have been made so far, as the suspects escaped through the dense forest and the interstate border.

title: 16 Killed After Bus, Fuel Tanker Collide In Indonesia
content: Four people were hospitalised, three of them with serious burns, according to another emergency official.

title: "Our Was An Ideological Alliance": Kanimozhi As Congress Ditches DMK For TVK
content: The Congress has said it would back Vijay's TVK, the newly formed party which made a blockbuster debut in the Tamil Nadu assembly elections but failed to get the majority.

title: 'Killed, Buried Wife And Daughter': Gujarat Man Dies By Suicide, Leaves Note
content: Girish allegedly confessed to killing his wife, Priyanka and their young daughter, Pari, before burying their bodies inside his home.

title: Pawan Kalyan backs Peacock Tarantula conservation as species faces extinction risk
content: The TOI Lifestyle Desk is a dynamic team of dedicated journalists who, with unwavering passion and commitment, sift through the pulse of the nation to curate a vibrant tapestry of lifestyle news for The Times of India readers. title: After poll result, ED changes gear, trouble seems headed TMC’s way
content: ED will question Sujit Bose and Rathin Ghosh, both ministers in the outgoing Mamata govt, in connection with its investigation into the municipality recruitment ‘scam’ case . this will be the second round of questioning of bose — fire minister defeated in his stronghold Bidhannagar — and the food minister who won from Madhyamgram . 'i will not resign', mamata alleges EC Bias, Says

title: Bengal woman's long journey: From domestic help to assembly
content: domestic help-turned-BJP MLA says she won't eat anything today . "i had barely eaten and was hungry after a day of high tension," she says . the legislator caught up on washing clothes and other household chores after weeks of being busy with the election .

title: Hantavirus that killed 3 on cruise ship capable of human transmission: 10 things to know
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. title: Wave of change rolls over three states, sweeps away two CMs, 50 ministers
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Ceasefire in a page: US, Iran shrink war to a memo
content: a draft document is closer to finalisation than at any point since hostilities began in late february . leaks about the one-page memo have come from only Washington, with Iran remaining circumspect . the draft memo could open the door to longer-term negotiations and end the global ordeal .

title: Over 68 lakh cyberattack attempts on counting day: EC
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996.
